examples: Quarrelsome, erratic and libidinous, Pushkin had affairs, fought duels and challenged authority wherever he went.
Unlike those for car insurance, the premiums are flat rates: the libidinous will pay the same as the uxorious.
His most popular characters, the doggedly determined Mickey Mouse and the libidinous Donald Duck, represented the ordinary man's ability to triumph over any adversity, even a Great Depression.
